name: Insurance Policy Hierarchy
scenario: You're building the policy management backend for an insurance company (think PolicyBazaar, HDFC Life, Bajaj Allianz). Insurance products are hierarchical: all policies share common data (policy number, holder name, premium), but Health Insurance adds coverage limit and deductible, Car Insurance adds vehicle details and own-damage coverage, and Life Insurance adds sum assured and nominee. Each type calculates its annual cost differently. This mirrors exactly how real insurance software models their product catalog.
description: Build an insurance policy hierarchy: InsurancePolicy (base), HealthInsurance, CarInsurance, LifeInsurance. Demonstrate inheritance, super(), method overriding, and polymorphic arrays.

core.description: `InsurancePolicy` base class: static auto-policyNumber, holderName, basePremium. Validate in constructor. toString, equals (by policyNumber), hashCode.
InsurancePolicy: `annualCost()` returns basePremium × 12. `getPolicySummary()` returns formatted string.
`HealthInsurance extends InsurancePolicy`: coverageLimit (double), deductible (double). Override annualCost(): base + deductible. Override getPolicySummary() using super.
`CarInsurance extends InsurancePolicy`: vehicleModel (String), ownDamageCoverage (double). Override annualCost(): base + percentage of ownDamageCoverage (0.5%). Override getPolicySummary().
`LifeInsurance extends InsurancePolicy`: sumAssured (double), nomineeName (String). Override annualCost(): base (term life is typically fixed premium). Override getPolicySummary().
main(): create one of each type, store in InsurancePolicy[], print all summaries and total annual costs.

bonus.description: Add `PolicyManager` class with InsurancePolicy[] and methods: addPolicy, getTotalAnnualCost (sums all), findMostExpensive.
Override equals in each subclass to also check type-specific fields (HealthInsurance equals checks coverageLimit too).
Add `applyDiscount(double percent)` to InsurancePolicy that reduces basePremium.

data_to_use:
Create:
- HealthInsurance for Aisha: monthly ₹2000, coverage ₹500000, deductible ₹5000
- CarInsurance for Raj: monthly ₹1500, vehicle Honda City, own-damage ₹800000
- LifeInsurance for Priya: monthly ₹3000, sum assured ₹5000000, nominee 'Rahul'

Expected annual costs:
- Health: 2000×12 + 5000 = 29000
- Car: 1500×12 + 800000×0.005 = 18000 + 4000 = 22000
- Life: 3000×12 = 36000
example_output:
=== Insurance Policies ===
Health Policy #1 [Aisha] — ₹2000/mo, Coverage: ₹500000, Deductible: ₹5000 | Annual: ₹29000.0
Car Policy #2 [Raj] — ₹1500/mo, Vehicle: Honda City, Own-Damage: ₹800000 | Annual: ₹22000.0
Life Policy #3 [Priya] — ₹3000/mo, Sum Assured: ₹5000000, Nominee: Rahul | Annual: ₹36000.0
Total annual cost: ₹87000.0

criteria: annualCost() and getPolicySummary() base implementations (10 pts)
HealthInsurance: correct super(), fields, overrides (13 pts)
CarInsurance: correct super(), fields, overrides (13 pts)
LifeInsurance: correct super(), fields, overrides (14 pts)
Loop correctly calls each policy's overridden methods (10 pts)
toString, equals, hashCode in base (10 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Use super() in subclass constructors and super.getPolicySummary() in overrides. The annualCost formula for each type is in the requirements.
submission_instructions: Save as InsuranceSystem.java. Run it. Push to GitHub — this models a real insurance product catalog hierarchy.
tips_for_success: InsurancePolicy base class should be complete (all Object methods) before writing subclasses.
Each subclass: super(holderName, basePremium) first in constructor.
getPolicySummary() in each subclass: start with super.getPolicySummary() + add type-specific info.
annualCost(): each subclass overrides with its formula — Health adds deductible, Car adds own-damage %.
For the loop: InsurancePolicy[] holds all types; each .annualCost() calls the right override.
what_youll_learn_by_doing_this: Insurance product hierarchies are a classic OOP domain. PolicyBazaar, HDFC Life, Bajaj Allianz — all have product catalogs structured exactly like this. The annualCost() override mirrors how different products price differently while sharing a common interface. Most importantly: you'll feel the power of the InsurancePolicy[] loop — one line of code that works for all three types, calling the right calculation for each. That's the inheritance + polymorphism payoff.
extensions_for_advanced_learners: Add TravelInsurance with destination and duration fields
Make InsurancePolicy abstract (Topic 5.3 preview — abstract annualCost())
Add a claim() method to each type with type-specific claim processing
Add renewal logic: expired policies get a 5% premium increase
Build a PolicyComparator that sorts policies by annual cost
